conflicts.description: The previous sheet treated Delroy's renewal-money theft and his Devon remittances as if Tiana learned both by chapter_08, but the manuscript only gives her the remittance receipts in chapter_13.
conflicts.canonical_value: Tiana learns the spent renewal money in chapter_08 and the Devon remittances in chapter_13.
conflicts.resolution_note: Chapter_08 only names the renewal envelope; chapter_13 is the first time the manuscript puts the remittance receipts in Tiana's hands.

conflicts.description: The previous sheet's five-month span is too long for the manuscript's explicit month markers.
conflicts.canonical_value: About three months, from June 2019 into late-summer storm season.
conflicts.resolution_note: The prose explicitly tracks June, July, and August, then places chapter_18 two weeks before classes start, so the prior estimate overstated the span.

conflicts.description: Claude schedules Delroy for an old medical building off Twenty-Seventh in chapter_09, but chapter_10's actual demolition sequence is fully staged at Palm Court Apartments, an old fourplex off Seventy-First.
conflicts.canonical_value: Palm Court Apartments, an old fourplex off Seventy-First.
conflicts.resolution_note: Chapter_10 embeds the fourplex location through pickup, site briefing, foreman assignment, and injury mechanics, so it is more deeply built into the causal structure.

conflicts.description: Chapter_13 summarizes the remittance slips as 60, 80, 45, and 100, but chapter_14 lays out 45, 60, 50, 40, 75, plus a $270 transfer from last Friday.
conflicts.canonical_value: Delroy made multiple remittances to Devon since June, including a $270 transfer the Friday before chapter_14; the smaller earlier amounts are not canonicalized.
conflicts.resolution_note: Chapter_14 is the confrontation scene where the receipts are physically laid out. To avoid preserving inconsistent minor figures, the updated sheet keeps only the stable continuity fact.
